content.content: 690 /Users/lswhiteh/.claude/hooks/peon-ping/peon.sh

1→#!/bin/bash
     2→# peon-ping: Warcraft III Peon voice lines for Claude Code hooks
     3→# Replaces notify.sh — handles sounds, tab titles, and notifications
     4→set -uo pipefail
     5→
     6→# --- Platform detection ---
     7→detect_platform() {
     8→  case "$(uname -s)" in
     9→    Darwin) echo "mac" ;;
    10→    Linux)
    11→      if grep -qi microsoft /proc/version 2>/dev/null; then
    12→        echo "wsl"
    13→      else
    14→        echo "linux"
    15→      fi ;;
    16→    *) echo "unknown" ;;
    17→  esac
    18→}
    19→PLATFORM=${PLATFORM:-$(detect_platform)}
    20→
    21→PEON_DIR="${CLAUDE_PEON_DIR:-$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)}"
    22→CONFIG="$PEON_DIR/config.json"
    23→STATE="$PEON_DIR/.state.json"
    24→
    25→# --- Linux audio backend detection ---
    26→detect_linux_player() {
    27→  # Helper to check if a player is available (respects test-mode disable markers)
    28→  player_available() {
    29→    local cmd="$1"
    30→    command -v "$cmd" &>/dev/null || return 1
    31→    # In test mode, check for disable marker
    32→    [ "${PEON_TEST:-0}" = "1" ] && [ -f "${CLAUDE_PEON_DIR}/.disabled_${cmd}" ] && return 1
    33→    return 0
    34→  }
    35→
    36→  if player_available pw-play; then
    37→    echo "pw-play"
    38→  elif player_available paplay; then
    39→    echo "paplay"
    40→  elif player_available ffplay; then
    41→    echo "ffplay"
    42→  elif player_available mpv; then
    43→    echo "mpv"
    44→  elif player_available play; then
    45→    echo "play"
    46→  elif player_available aplay; then
    47→    echo "aplay"
    48→  else
    49→    # Warn only once per process to avoid spam
    50→    if [ -z "${WARNED_NO_LINUX_AUDIO_BACKEND:-}" ]; then
    51→      echo "WARNING: No audio backend found. Please install one of: pw-play, paplay, ffplay, mpv, play (SoX), or aplay" >&2
    52→      WARNED_NO_LINUX_AUDIO_BACKEND=1
    53→    fi
    54→    return 1
    55→  fi
    56→}
    57→
    58→# --- Linux audio playback with backend-specific volume handling ---
    59→play_linux_sound() {
    60→  local file="$1" vol="$2" player="$3"
    61→
    62→  # Skip playback if no backend available
    63→  [ -z "$player" ] && return 0
    64→
    65→  # Background mode: use nohup & for async playback (default)
    66→  # Synchronous mode: no nohup/& for tests (when PEON_TEST=1)
    67→  local use_bg=true
    68→  [ "${PEON_TEST:-0}" = "1" ] && use_bg=false
    69→
    70→  case "$player" in
    71→    pw-play)
    72→      # pw-play (PipeWire) expects volume as float 0.0-1.0 (unlike paplay 0-65536, ffplay/mpv 0-100)
    73→      if [ "$use_bg" = true ]; then
    74→        nohup pw-play --volume "$vol" "$file" >/dev/null 2>&1 &
    75→      else
    76→        pw-play --volume "$vol" "$file" >/dev/null 2>&1
    77→      fi
    78→      ;;
    79→    paplay)
    80→      local pa_vol
    81→      pa_vol=$(python3 -c "print(max(0, min(65536, int($vol * 65536))))")
    82→      if [ "$use_bg" = true ]; then
    83→        nohup paplay --volume="$pa_vol" "$file" >/dev/null 2>&1 &
    84→      else
    85→        paplay --volume="$pa_vol" "$file" >/dev/null 2>&1
    86→      fi
    87→      ;;
    88→    ffplay)
    89→      local ff_vol
    90→      ff_vol=$(python3 -c "print(max(0, min(100, int($vol * 100))))")
    91→      if [ "$use_bg" = true ]; then
    92→        nohup ffplay -nodisp -autoexit -volume "$ff_vol" "$file" >/dev/null 2>&1 &
    93→      else
    94→        ffplay -nodisp -autoexit -volume "$ff_vol" "$file" >/dev/null 2>&1
    95→      fi
    96→      ;;
    97→    mpv)
    98→      local mpv_vol
    99→      mpv_vol=$(python3 -c "print(max(0, min(100, int($vol * 100))))")
   100→      if [ "$use_bg" = true ]; then
   101→        nohup mpv --no-video --volume="$mpv_vol" "$file" >/dev/null 2>&1 &
   102→      else
   103→        mpv --no-video --volume="$mpv_vol" "$file" >/dev/null 2>&1
   104→      fi
   105→      ;;
   106→    play)
   107→      if [ "$use_bg" = true ]; then
   108→        nohup play -v "$vol" "$file" >/dev/null 2>&1 &
   109→      else
   110→        play -v "$vol" "$file" >/dev/null 2>&1
   111→      fi
   112→      ;;
   113→    aplay)
   114→      if [ "$use_bg" = true ]; then
   115→        nohup aplay -q "$file" >/dev/null 2>&1 &
   116→      else
   117→        aplay -q "$file" >/dev/null 2>&1
   118→      fi
   119→      ;;
   120→  esac
   121→}
   122→
   123→# --- Platform-aware audio playback ---
   124→play_sound() {
   125→  local file="$1" vol="$2"
   126→  case "$PLATFORM" in
   127→    mac)
   128→      nohup afplay -v "$vol" "$file" >/dev/null 2>&1 &
   129→      ;;
   130→    wsl)
   131→      local wpath
   132→      wpath=$(wslpath -w "$file")
   133→      # Convert backslashes to forward slashes for file:/// URI
   134→      wpath="${wpath//\\//}"
   135→      powershell.exe -NoProfile -NonInteractive -Command "
   136→        Add-Type -AssemblyName PresentationCore
   137→        \$p = New-Object System.Windows.Media.MediaPlayer
   138→        \$p.Open([Uri]::new('file:///$wpath'))
   139→        \$p.Volume = $vol
   140→        Start-Sleep -Milliseconds 200
   141→        \$p.Play()
   142→        Start-Sleep -Seconds 3
   143→        \$p.Close()
   144→      " &>/dev/null &
   145→      ;;
   146→    linux)
   147→      local player
   148→      player=$(detect_linux_player) || player=""
   149→      if [ -n "$player" ]; then
   150→        play_linux_sound "$file" "$vol" "$player"
   151→      fi
   152→      ;;
   153→  esac
   154→}
   155→
   156→# --- Platform-aware notification ---
   157→# Args: msg, title, color (red/blue/yellow)
   158→send_notification() {
   159→  local msg="$1" title="$2" color="${3:-red}"
   160→  case "$PLATFORM" in
   161→    mac)
   162→      # Use terminal-native escape sequences where supported (shows terminal icon).
   163→      # Falls back to osascript which attributes notifications to Script Editor.
   164→      case "${TERM_PROGRAM:-}" in
   165→        iTerm.app)
   166→          # iTerm2 OSC 9 — notification with iTerm2 icon
   167→          printf '\e]9;%s\007' "$title: $msg" 2>/dev/null
   168→          ;;
   169→        kitty)
   170→          # Kitty OSC 99
   171→          printf '\e]99;i=peon:d=0;%s\e\\' "$title: $msg" 2>/dev/null
   172→          ;;
   173→        *)
   174→          # Terminal.app, Warp, Ghostty, etc. — no native escape; use osascript
   175→          nohup osascript - "$msg" "$title" >/dev/null 2>&1 <<'APPLESCRIPT' &
   176→on run argv
   177→  display notification (item 1 of argv) with title (item 2 of argv)
   178→end run
   179→APPLESCRIPT
   180→          ;;
   181→      esac
   182→      ;;
   183→    wsl)
   184→      # Map color name to RGB
   185→      local rgb_r=180 rgb_g=0 rgb_b=0
   186→      case "$color" in
   187→        blue)   rgb_r=30  rgb_g=80  rgb_b=180 ;;
   188→        yellow) rgb_r=200 rgb_g=160 rgb_b=0   ;;
   189→        red)    rgb_r=180 rgb_g=0   rgb_b=0   ;;
   190→      esac
   191→      (
   192→        # Claim a popup slot for vertical stacking
   193→        slot_dir="/tmp/peon-ping-popups"
   194→        mkdir -p "$slot_dir"
   195→        slot=0
   196→        while ! mkdir "$slot_dir/slot-$slot" 2>/dev/null; do
   197→          slot=$((slot + 1))
   198→        done
   199→        y_offset=$((40 + slot * 90))
   200→        powershell.exe -NoProfile -NonInteractive -Command "

#!/bin/bash
# claude-agent-runner — Polls Linear for issues labeled "agent", spawns isolated Claude instances
#
# Usage:
#   claude-agent-runner                           Run for all configured workspaces/teams
#   claude-agent-runner --auto-review             Run + auto-review PRs after creation
#   claude-agent-runner --auto-fix                Run + auto-review + auto-fix loop
#   claude-agent-runner --sequenced               Only pick up issues whose blockers are done
#   claude-agent-runner --pipeline <file.md>      Parse requirements into Linear issues
#   claude-agent-runner --spec <idea.md>          Generate spec from idea, optionally create issues
#   claude-agent-runner --review-pr <num> --repo <path>  Standalone PR review
#   claude-agent-runner --cleanup                 Clean up merged worktrees
#   claude-agent-runner --status                  Show active agents and worktrees
#   claude-agent-runner --help                    Show usage
#
# Config: ~/.config/claude-agents/config.json
# Logs:   ~/.config/claude-agents/logs/

# Requires: jq, claude, git, gh, curl

    52→# Idempotent: skips if command path already present under the event.
    53→register_hook() {

    55→  local MATCHER="$2"

    58→  local HOOK_PATH="$HOOKS_DIR/$HOOK_FILE"

    60→  # Check if already registered under this event

    62→    echo "  Skipped: $HOOK_FILE already registered under $EVENT"

    64→  fi
    65→
    66→  # Build the hook entry
    67→  local HOOK_OBJ
    68→  if [ "$ASYNC" = "true" ]; then
    69→    HOOK_OBJ=$(jq -n --arg cmd "$HOOK_PATH" '{type: "command", command: $cmd, async: true}')
    70→  else
    71→    HOOK_OBJ=$(jq -n --arg cmd "$HOOK_PATH" '{type: "command", command: $cmd}')
    72→  fi
    73→
    74→  # Check if a matcher group already exists for this event+matcher
    75→  local MATCHER_EXISTS
    76→  MATCHER_EXISTS=$(jq -e ".hooks.\"$EVENT\"[]? | select(.matcher == \"$MATCHER\")" "$SETTINGS" 2>/dev/null || true)

    80→    jq "(.hooks.\"$EVENT\"[] | select(.matcher == \"$MATCHER\") | .hooks) += [$HOOK_OBJ]" \
    81→      "$SETTINGS" > "$SETTINGS.tmp" && mv "$SETTINGS.tmp" "$SETTINGS"
    82→  else

    87→      "$SETTINGS" > "$SETTINGS.tmp" && mv "$SETTINGS.tmp" "$SETTINGS"
    88→  fi
    89→
    90→  echo "  Registered $HOOK_FILE under $EVENT (matcher: ${MATCHER:-\"(all)\"})"
    91→}
    92→

   104→

#!/bin/bash
# setup.sh — Install claude-agent-runner components
# Usage: git clone https://github.com/Lswhiteh/claude-agent-runner.git && cd claude-agent-runner && ./setup.sh

set -euo pipefail

REPO_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"

echo "Setting up claude-agent-runner from: $REPO_DIR"

# --- bin/ → ~/.local/bin/ ---
echo ""
echo "=== Scripts ==="
mkdir -p "$HOME/.local/bin"
for script in "$REPO_DIR/bin/"*; do
  [ -f "$script" ] || continue
  NAME=$(basename "$script")
  TARGET="$HOME/.local/bin/$NAME"
  if [ -L "$TARGET" ] || [ -f "$TARGET" ]; then
    echo "  Replacing: $TARGET"
    rm -f "$TARGET"
  fi
  ln -s "$script" "$TARGET"
  chmod +x "$script"
  echo "  Linked: $NAME → $TARGET"
done

# --- hooks/ → ~/.claude/hooks/ ---
echo ""
echo "=== Hooks ==="
mkdir -p "$HOME/.claude/hooks"
for hook in "$REPO_DIR/hooks/"*; do
  [ -f "$hook" ] || continue
  NAME=$(basename "$hook")
  TARGET="$HOME/.claude/hooks/$NAME"
  if [ -L "$TARGET" ] || [ -f "$TARGET" ]; then
    echo "  Replacing: $TARGET"
    rm -f "$TARGET"
  fi
  ln -s "$hook" "$TARGET"
  chmod +x "$hook"
  echo "  Linked: $NAME → $TARGET"
done

# Register hooks in settings.json (correct nested format)
SETTINGS="$HOME/.claude/settings.json"
HOOKS_DIR="$HOME/.claude/hooks"

# register_hook <event> <matcher> <hook_file> [async]
# Adds a hook entry to settings.json using the correct nested format:
#   hooks.<Event>[{matcher, hooks:[{type, command}]}]
# Idempotent: skips if command path already present under the event.
register_hook() {
  local EVENT="$1"
  local MATCHER="$2"
  local HOOK_FILE="$3"
  local ASYNC="${4:-false}"
  local HOOK_PATH="$HOOKS_DIR/$HOOK_FILE"

  # Check if already registered under this event
  if jq -e ".hooks.\"$EVENT\"[]?.hooks[]? | select(.command == \"$HOOK_PATH\")" "$SETTINGS" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "  Skipped: $HOOK_FILE already registered under $EVENT"
    return 0
  fi

  # Build the hook entry
  local HOOK_OBJ
  if [ "$ASYNC" = "true" ]; then
    HOOK_OBJ=$(jq -n --arg cmd "$HOOK_PATH" '{type: "command", command: $cmd, async: true}')
  else
    HOOK_OBJ=$(jq -n --arg cmd "$HOOK_PATH" '{type: "command", command: $cmd}')
  fi

  # Check if a matcher group already exists for this event+matcher
  local MATCHER_EXISTS
  MATCHER_EXISTS=$(jq -e ".hooks.\"$EVENT\"[]? | select(.matcher == \"$MATCHER\")" "$SETTINGS" 2>/dev/null || true)

  if [ -n "$MATCHER_EXISTS" ]; then
    # Append to existing matcher group's hooks array
    jq "(.hooks.\"$EVENT\"[] | select(.matcher == \"$MATCHER\") | .hooks) += [$HOOK_OBJ]" \
      "$SETTINGS" > "$SETTINGS.tmp" && mv "$SETTINGS.tmp" "$SETTINGS"
  else
    # Create new matcher group
    local GROUP
    GROUP=$(jq -n --arg m "$MATCHER" --argjson h "[$HOOK_OBJ]" '{matcher: $m, hooks: $h}')
    jq ".hooks.\"$EVENT\" = (.hooks.\"$EVENT\" // []) + [$GROUP]" \
      "$SETTINGS" > "$SETTINGS.tmp" && mv "$SETTINGS.tmp" "$SETTINGS"
  fi

  echo "  Registered $HOOK_FILE under $EVENT (matcher: ${MATCHER:-\"(all)\"})"
}

if [ -f "$SETTINGS" ] && command -v jq &>/dev/null; then
  # Ensure hooks object exists
  if ! jq -e '.hooks' "$SETTINGS" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    jq '. + {hooks: {}}' "$SETTINGS" > "$SETTINGS.tmp" && mv "$SETTINGS.tmp" "$SETTINGS"
  fi

  # Migrate old flat-format hooks if present (pre-v2 format)
  if jq -e '.hooks | type == "array"' "$SETTINGS" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "  Migrating old flat-format hooks to nested format..."
    jq '.hooks = {}' "$SETTINGS" > "$SETTINGS.tmp" && mv "$SETTINGS.tmp" "$SETTINGS"
  fi

  # --- PreToolUse:Bash hooks ---
  register_hook "PreToolUse" "Bash" "block-destructive.sh"
  register_hook "PreToolUse" "Bash" "scope-guard.sh"
  register_hook "PreToolUse" "Bash" "command-budget.sh"
  register_hook "PreToolUse" "Bash" "command-rewriter.sh"

  # --- PostToolUse:Edit|Write (async) ---
  register_hook "PostToolUse" "Edit|Write" "auto-lint.sh" "true"

  # --- PostToolUseFailure:Bash ---
  register_hook "PostToolUseFailure" "Bash" "rtk-failure-hint.sh"

  # --- SessionStart ---
  register_hook "SessionStart" "" "session-context.sh"

  # --- Stop ---
  register_hook "Stop" "" "stop-gate.sh"

  # --- SubagentStop ---
  register_hook "SubagentStop" "" "subagent-collector.sh"

  # --- TaskCompleted ---
  register_hook "TaskCompleted" "" "task-validator.sh"

  # --- PreCompact ---
  register_hook "PreCompact" "" "pre-compact.sh"
else
  echo "  WARNING: Could not register hooks — settings.json not found or jq not installed"
  echo "  Create ~/.claude/settings.json with {\"hooks\": {}} and re-run setup.sh"
fi

# --- skills/ → ~/.claude/skills/ ---
echo ""
echo "=== Skills ==="
mkdir -p "$HOME/.claude/skills"
for skill_dir in "$REPO_DIR/skills/"*/; do
  [ -d "$skill_dir" ] || continue
  NAME=$(basename "$skill_dir")
  TARGET="$HOME/.claude/skills/$NAME"
  if [ -L "$TARGET" ]; then
    rm -f "$TARGET"
  elif [ -d "$TARGET" ]; then
    echo "  Skipped: $TARGET already exists (not a symlink)"
    continue
  fi
  ln -s "$skill_dir" "$TARGET"
  echo "  Linked: $NAME → $TARGET"
done

# --- config/ → ~/.config/claude-agents/ ---
echo ""
echo "=== Config ==="
mkdir -p "$HOME/.config/claude-agents"

CONFIG_TARGET="$HOME/.config/claude-agents/config.json"
if [ ! -f "$CONFIG_TARGET" ]; then
  cp "$REPO_DIR/config/config.example.json" "$CONFIG_TARGET"
  echo "  Created: $CONFIG_TARGET (from example — edit with your repos)"
else
  echo "  Skipped: $CONFIG_TARGET already exists"
fi

mkdir -p "$HOME/.config/claude-agents/logs"
mkdir -p "$HOME/.config/claude-agents/locks"
mkdir -p "$HOME/.config/claude-agents/traces"

# Create secrets.env placeholder if missing
SECRETS_FILE="$HOME/.config/claude-agents/secrets.env"
if [ ! -f "$SECRETS_FILE" ]; then
  cat > "$SECRETS_FILE" << 'SECRETS'
# Claude Agent Runner secrets — fill in your API keys
# This file is sourced by the agent runner and cron jobs
# Permissions: 600 (owner-only read/write)

# export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...
# export LINEAR_API_KEY_EXAMPLE=lin_api_...
# export SLACK_WEBHOOK_EXAMPLE=https://hooks.slack.com/services/...
SECRETS
  chmod 600 "$SECRETS_FILE"
  echo "  Created: $SECRETS_FILE (fill in your API keys)"
else
  echo "  Skipped: $SECRETS_FILE already exists"
fi

# --- summary ---
echo ""
echo "=== Done! ==="
echo ""
echo "What was set up:"
echo "  ~/.local/bin/              ← claude-agent-runner, ci-gate"
echo "  ~/.claude/hooks/           ← 11 hooks (guardrails, linting, budget, context, ...)"
echo "  ~/.claude/skills/          ← agent skills (implement, orchestrate, scoped-worker, ...)"
echo "  ~/.config/claude-agents/   ← config, secrets, logs, locks"
echo ""
echo "Remaining manual steps:"
echo ""
echo "  1. Fill in API keys:"
echo "     nano ~/.config/claude-agents/secrets.env"
echo ""
echo "  2. Edit agent config with your repo paths:"
echo "     nano ~/.config/claude-agents/config.json"
echo ""
echo "  3. Optional: Install the cron job for autonomous agents:"
echo "     crontab -l 2>/dev/null | cat - <<'CRON' | crontab -"
echo "     PATH=/usr/local/bin:/usr/bin:/bin:\$HOME/.local/bin:/opt/homebrew/bin"
echo "     */5 * * * * source \$HOME/.config/claude-agents/secrets.env && \$HOME/.local/bin/claude-agent-runner >> \$HOME/.config/claude-agents/cron.log 2>&1"
echo "     CRON"
echo ""

file.content:
#!/bin/bash
# agent-trace — CLI tool for querying JSONL event traces from claude-agent-runner
#
# Usage:
#   agent-trace list                    List all trace files with event counts and time range
#   agent-trace show <identifier>       Pretty-print events (jq colored)
#   agent-trace tail <identifier>       Live-tail events as they're emitted
#   agent-trace view [identifier]       Start HTTP viewer in browser
#   agent-trace help                    Show this help
#
# Requires: jq (for show)

set -uo pipefail

TRACES_DIR="$HOME/.config/claude-agents/traces"

# Resolve viewer HTML path (sibling to this script)
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
VIEWER_HTML="$SCRIPT_DIR/agent-trace-viewer.html"

usage() {
  cat <<'EOF'
agent-trace — Query JSONL event traces from claude-agent-runner

Usage:
  agent-trace list                    List all trace files with event counts and time range
  agent-trace show <identifier>       Pretty-print events (jq colored)
  agent-trace tail <identifier>       Live-tail events as they're emitted
  agent-trace view [identifier]       Start HTTP viewer in browser
  agent-trace help                    Show this help

# ci-gate — Deterministic CI checks that MUST pass before push/PR
# Called by claude-agent-runner before opening PRs.
# Also usable standalone: ci-gate [repo-path]
#
# Exit 0 = all checks pass, safe to push
# Exit 1 = checks failed, do NOT push
#
# Looks for a .ci-gate file in the repo root for project-specific commands.
# Falls back to sensible defaults if none exists.

--workspace) PIPELINE_WS="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --team) PIPELINE_TEAM="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --dry-run) PIPELINE_DRY_RUN=true; shift ;;
        *) shift ;;
      esac
    done
    if [ -z "$PIPELINE_FILE" ]; then
      echo "Usage: claude-agent-runner --pipeline <requirements.md> [--workspace <name>] [--team <name>] [--dry-run]"
      exit 1
    fi
    run_pipeline "$PIPELINE_FILE" "$PIPELINE_WS" "$PIPELINE_TEAM" "$PIPELINE_DRY_RUN"
    ;;
  --spec)
    shift
    SPEC_FILE="${1:-}"
    shift || true
    SPEC_WS=""
    SPEC_TEAM=""
    SPEC_INTERACTIVE=false
    SPEC_CREATE=false
    while [ $# -gt 0 ]; do
      case "$1" in
        --workspace) SPEC_WS="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --team) SPEC_TEAM="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --interactive) SPEC_INTERACTIVE=true; shift ;;
        --create) SPEC_CREATE=true; shift ;;
        *) shift ;;
      esac
    done
    if [ -z "$SPEC_FILE" ]; then
      echo "Usage: claude-agent-runner --spec <idea.md> [--workspace <name>] [--team <name>] [--interactive] [--create]"
      exit 1
    fi
    run_spec "$SPEC_FILE" "$SPEC_WS" "$SPEC_TEAM" "$SPEC_INTERACTIVE" "$SPEC_CREATE"
    ;;
  --review-pr)
    shift
    REVIEW_PR_NUM="${1:-}"
    shift || true
    REVIEW_REPO=""
    while [ $# -gt 0 ]; do
      case "$1" in
        --repo) REVIEW_REPO="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        *) shift ;;
      esac
    done
    if [ -z "$REVIEW_PR_NUM" ] || [ -z "$REVIEW_REPO" ]; then
      echo "Usage: claude-agent-runner --review-pr <number> --repo <path>"
      exit 1
    fi
    cd "$REVIEW_REPO" || { echo "ERROR: Repo not found: $REVIEW_REPO"; exit 1; }
    BRANCH=$(gh pr view "$REVIEW_PR_NUM" --json headRefName --jq '.headRefName' 2>/dev/null)
    if [ -z "$BRANCH" ]; then
      echo "ERROR: PR #${REVIEW_PR_NUM} not found"
      exit 1
    fi
    auto_review_pr "$REVIEW_REPO" "$BRANCH" "PR-${REVIEW_PR_NUM}"
    ;;
  --cleanup)
    jq -r '.workspaces | to_entries[] | .value.teams | to_entries[] | .value.repo' "$CONFIG" | while IFS= read -r repo; do
      cleanup_worktrees "$repo"
    done
    ;;
  --status)
    show_status
    ;;
  --help|-h)
    echo "claude-agent-runner — Autonomous Claude agents from Linear issues"
    echo ""
    echo "Usage:"
    echo "  claude-agent-runner                                   Poll all workspaces (default)"
    echo "  claude-agent-runner --auto-review                     Poll + auto-review PRs"
    echo "  claude-agent-runner --auto-fix                        Poll + auto-review + fix loop"
    echo "  claude-agent-runner --sequenced                       Only pick up unblocked issues"
    echo "  claude-agent-runner --pipeline <file.md> [options]    Parse requirements → Linear"
    echo "  claude-agent-runner --spec <idea.md> [options]        Generate spec from idea"
    echo "  claude-agent-runner --review-pr <num> --repo <path>   Standalone PR review"
    echo "  claude-agent-runner --cleanup                         Clean up merged worktrees"
    echo "  claude-agent-runner --status                          Show active agents"
    echo "  claude-agent-runner --help                            Show this help"
    echo ""
    echo "Pipeline options:"
    echo "  --workspace <name>   Target workspace (default: first in config)"
    echo "  --team <name>        Target team (default: first in workspace)"
    echo "  --dry-run            Print parsed stories without creating issues"

content.content:
1→# claude-agent-runner
     2→
     3→Autonomous Claude Code agent orchestrator. Polls Linear for labeled issues, spawns isolated Claude instances in git worktrees, runs CI gates, and opens GitHub PRs — fully hands-off.
     4→
     5→## Features
     6→
     7→- **Linear-driven**: Label an issue → agent picks it up, implements, opens a PR
     8→- **Isolated worktrees**: Each agent works in its own git worktree (outside the repo to avoid bundler conflicts)
     9→- **Docker DB isolation**: Per-agent Postgres containers with random ephemeral ports
    10→- **CI gate**: Deterministic checks (typecheck, test, build) with auto-fix retries
    11→- **Feedback resume**: Re-label an issue to resume with PR review feedback
    12→- **Auto-review**: Spawns a read-only Claude to review PRs with structured feedback
    13→- **Auto-fix loop**: Iterates review → fix → CI → push until approved (configurable max)
    14→- **Orchestration**: Multi-agent decomposition — add the "Orchestrate" label to break a ticket into scoped subtasks with a supervisor
    15→- **Scoped workers**: File-scope enforcement via hook — workers can only write to their assigned files
    16→- **Pipeline**: Parse a requirements markdown into Linear issues with dependency chains
    17→- **Spec generation**: Turn a free-form idea into structured requirements with `--spec`
    18→- **Dependency sequencing**: Only pick up issues whose blockers are completed
    19→- **Guardrails**: Hooks block destructive commands and enforce file scope in agent mode
    20→- **Multi-workspace**: Support multiple Linear workspaces and teams
    21→- **Slack notifications**: Optional webhook notifications at key lifecycle points
    22→- **Event tracing**: JSONL traces per issue with CLI viewer and browser timeline
    23→
    24→## Quick Start
    25→
    26→```bash
    27→git clone https://github.com/Lswhiteh/claude-agent-runner.git
    28→cd claude-agent-runner
    29→./setup.sh
    30→```
    31→
    32→Then:
    33→1. Edit `~/.config/claude-agents/secrets.env` with your API keys
    34→2. Edit `~/.config/claude-agents/config.json` with your repos
    35→3. Label a Linear issue with "Agent" and run `claude-agent-runner`
    36→
    37→## Usage
    38→
    39→```bash
    40→# Poll all workspaces (default)
    41→claude-agent-runner
    42→
    43→# Poll with auto-review of created PRs
    44→claude-agent-runner --auto-review
    45→
    46→# Poll with auto-review + auto-fix loop
    47→claude-agent-runner --auto-fix
    48→
    49→# Only pick up issues with completed blockers
    50→claude-agent-runner --sequenced
    51→
    52→# Parse requirements into Linear issues
    53→claude-agent-runner --pipeline requirements.md --workspace myapp --team Engineering
    54→claude-agent-runner --pipeline requirements.md --dry-run
    55→
    56→# Generate spec from an idea, optionally create Linear issues
    57→claude-agent-runner --spec idea.md
    58→claude-agent-runner --spec idea.md --interactive
    59→claude-agent-runner --spec idea.md --create
    60→
    61→# Standalone PR review
    62→claude-agent-runner --review-pr 42 --repo /path/to/repo
    63→
    64→# Clean up merged worktrees
    65→claude-agent-runner --cleanup
    66→
    67→# Show active agents and pending issues
    68→claude-agent-runner --status
    69→
    70→# Query event traces
    71→agent-trace list                    # List all traces
    72→agent-trace show ENG-123            # Pretty-print a trace
    73→agent-trace tail ENG-123            # Live-tail events
    74→agent-trace view                    # Open browser timeline viewer
    75→```
    76→
    77→## Configuration
    78→
    79→`~/.config/claude-agents/config.json`:
    80→
    81→```json
    82→{
    83→  "label": "Agent",
    84→  "max_parallel": 3,
    85→  "max_turns": 15,
    86→  "max_ci_retries": 3,
    87→  "max_review_fix_iterations": 3,
    88→  "ci_gate_path": "",
    89→  "orchestrator": {
    90→    "enabled": false,
    91→    "label": "Orchestrate",
    92→    "max_subtasks": 8,
    93→    "max_orchestrator_turns": 50,
    94→    "poll_interval_seconds": 30,

file.content:
# claude-agent-runner

Autonomous Claude Code agent orchestrator. Polls Linear for labeled issues, spawns isolated Claude instances in git worktrees, runs CI gates, and opens GitHub PRs — fully hands-off.

## Features

- **Linear-driven**: Label an issue → agent picks it up, implements, opens a PR
- **Isolated worktrees**: Each agent works in its own git worktree (outside the repo to avoid bundler conflicts)
- **Docker DB isolation**: Per-agent Postgres containers with random ephemeral ports
- **CI gate**: Deterministic checks (typecheck, test, build) with auto-fix retries
- **Feedback resume**: Re-label an issue to resume with PR review feedback
- **Auto-review**: Spawns a read-only Claude to review PRs with structured feedback
- **Auto-fix loop**: Iterates review → fix → CI → push until approved (configurable max)
- **Orchestration**: Multi-agent decomposition — add the "Orchestrate" label to break a ticket into scoped subtasks with a supervisor
- **Scoped workers**: File-scope enforcement via hook — workers can only write to their assigned files
- **Pipeline**: Parse a requirements markdown into Linear issues with dependency chains
- **Spec generation**: Turn a free-form idea into structured requirements with `--spec`
- **Dependency sequencing**: Only pick up issues whose blockers are completed
- **Guardrails**: Hooks block destructive commands and enforce file scope in agent mode
- **Multi-workspace**: Support multiple Linear workspaces and teams
- **Slack notifications**: Optional webhook notifications at key lifecycle points
- **Event tracing**: JSONL traces per issue with CLI viewer and browser timeline

## Quick Start

```bash
git clone https://github.com/Lswhiteh/claude-agent-runner.git
cd claude-agent-runner
./setup.sh
```

Then:
1. Edit `~/.config/claude-agents/secrets.env` with your API keys
2. Edit `~/.config/claude-agents/config.json` with your repos
3. Label a Linear issue with "Agent" and run `claude-agent-runner`

## Usage

```bash
# Poll all workspaces (default)
claude-agent-runner

# Poll with auto-review of created PRs
claude-agent-runner --auto-review

# Poll with auto-review + auto-fix loop
claude-agent-runner --auto-fix

# Only pick up issues with completed blockers
claude-agent-runner --sequenced

# Parse requirements into Linear issues
claude-agent-runner --pipeline requirements.md --workspace myapp --team Engineering
claude-agent-runner --pipeline requirements.md --dry-run

# Generate spec from an idea, optionally create Linear issues
claude-agent-runner --spec idea.md
claude-agent-runner --spec idea.md --interactive
claude-agent-runner --spec idea.md --create

# Standalone PR review
claude-agent-runner --review-pr 42 --repo /path/to/repo

# Clean up merged worktrees
claude-agent-runner --cleanup

# Show active agents and pending issues
claude-agent-runner --status

# Query event traces
agent-trace list                    # List all traces
agent-trace show ENG-123            # Pretty-print a trace
agent-trace tail ENG-123            # Live-tail events
agent-trace view                    # Open browser timeline viewer
```

## Configuration

`~/.config/claude-agents/config.json`:

```json
{
  "label": "Agent",
  "max_parallel": 3,
  "max_turns": 15,
  "max_ci_retries": 3,
  "max_review_fix_iterations": 3,
  "ci_gate_path": "",
  "orchestrator": {
    "enabled": false,
    "label": "Orchestrate",
    "max_subtasks": 8,
    "max_orchestrator_turns": 50,
    "poll_interval_seconds": 30,
    "max_validation_retries": 2
  },
  "workspaces": {
    "myapp": {
      "api_key_env": "LINEAR_API_KEY_MYAPP",
      "slack_webhook_env": "SLACK_WEBHOOK_MYAPP",
      "teams": {
        "Engineering": {
          "repo": "/path/to/repo",
          "local_db": "postgres"
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

### Config Fields

| Field | Default | Description |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| `label` | `"Agent"` | Linear label that triggers agent pickup |
| `max_parallel` | `3` | Max concurrent agents per repo |
| `max_turns` | `15` | Max Claude turns per agent session |
| `max_ci_retries` | `3` | CI gate retry attempts with auto-fix |
| `max_review_fix_iterations` | `3` | Auto-fix loop iterations |
| `ci_gate_path` | `""` | Explicit path to ci-gate (auto-detected if empty) |

### Orchestrator Fields

| Field | Default | Description |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| `orchestrator.enabled` | `false` | Enable multi-agent orchestration |
| `orchestrator.label` | `"Orchestrate"` | Linear label that triggers orchestration |
| `orchestrator.max_subtasks` | `8` | Max subtasks per decomposition |
| `orchestrator.max_orchestrator_turns` | `50` | Claude turns for decomposition |
| `orchestrator.poll_interval_seconds` | `30` | Supervisor polling interval |
| `orchestrator.max_validation_retries` | `2` | Retries for failed subtask validation |

### Team Fields

| Field | Values | Description |
|-------|--------|-------------|
| `repo` | path | Absolute path to the git repo |
| `local_db` | `"postgres"`, `"supabase"`, omit | Docker DB type per agent |

### API Key Resolution

1. Config `api_key_env` → named env var
2. `LINEAR_API_KEY_<WORKSPACE>` (auto-derived, uppercased)
3. `LINEAR_API_KEY` (global fallback)

### Slack Webhook Resolution

1. Config `slack_webhook_env` → named env var
2. `SLACK_WEBHOOK_<WORKSPACE>` (auto-derived)
3. No fallback — silent if not configured

## How It Works

```
1. Label a Linear issue with "Agent"
2. Runner polls Linear, finds the issue
3. Creates isolated worktree + optional Docker DB
4. Spawns Claude with full issue context
5. Claude implements, writes tests, makes atomic commits
6. Runner runs CI gate (typecheck, test, build)
7. If CI fails: spawns Claude to fix, retries up to N times
8. Pushes branch, creates GitHub PR
9. Optional: auto-review → auto-fix loop
10. Posts implementation report to Linear
```

### Orchestrated Multi-Agent

For complex, multi-concern features:

```
1. Add "Agent" + "Orchestrate" labels to a Linear issue
2. Runner spawns an orchestrator Claude to decompose the issue
3. Orchestrator explores codebase, creates scoped subtask JSON files
4. Runner creates Linear sub-issues with file scope metadata
5. Sub-issues get "Agent" label → picked up as scoped workers
6. Workers implement within their file scope (enforced by scope-guard hook)
7. Orchestrator polls for completion, handles scope overflow
8. After all subtasks done: integration CI → combined PR
```

If the orchestrator decides the issue doesn't need decomposition (single-concern), it falls back to the standard single-agent flow automatically.

### Spec Generation

Turn ideas into actionable Linear issues:

```bash
# Generate structured requirements from a free-form idea
claude-agent-runner --spec idea.md

# Interactive mode — Claude asks clarifying questions
claude-agent-runner --spec idea.md --interactive

# Generate and create Linear issues in one step
claude-agent-runner --spec idea.md --create
```

### Feedback Resume

When a reviewer leaves feedback on the PR or Linear ticket:
1. Re-add the "Agent" label to the issue
2. Runner detects existing worktree + PR
3. Gathers feedback from GitHub (PR comments, reviews, inline comments) and Linear
4. Spawns Claude with full feedback context
5. Claude makes new commits (no amend/force-push)
6. Runner pushes to existing PR

### Blocking

If the agent can't proceed:
1. Agent writes questions to `.claude/agent-blocked/<ID>.md`
2. Runner posts questions to Linear
3. Developer replies on Linear
4. Re-add "Agent" label → agent resumes with answers

### Event Tracing

Every issue run emits structured JSONL events (~30 event types across 7 categories) to `~/.config/claude-agents/traces/`.

```bash
# CLI tool
agent-trace list                    # List all traces
agent-trace show ENG-123            # Pretty-print events for an issue
agent-trace tail ENG-123            # Live-tail (like tail -f)
agent-trace view                    # Open browser timeline viewer
agent-trace view ENG-123            # Open viewer filtered to one trace
```

The timeline viewer is a single-file HTML app (no build step) with color-coded events, filter chips, search, auto-refresh, and swimlane view for orchestrated issues.

### Status

```bash
claude-agent-runner --status
```

Shows all configured workspaces/teams, active agents with PIDs and issue IDs, and pending issue counts per team.

## Documentation

- [Walkthrough](docs/walkthrough.md) — linear narrative of the full system
- [Slide deck](docs/walkthrough-slides.html) — 20-slide HTML presentation (open in browser)

## Requirements

- `jq`, `git`, `gh` (GitHub CLI), `curl`
- `claude` (Claude Code CLI)
- `docker` (only if using `local_db`)
- `pnpm` (only for Node.js projects)

## License

MIT

#!/bin/bash
# claude-agent-runner — Polls Linear for issues labeled "agent", spawns isolated Claude instances
#
# Usage:
#   claude-agent-runner                           Run for all configured workspaces/teams
#   claude-agent-runner --auto-review             Run + auto-review PRs after creation
#   claude-agent-runner --auto-fix                Run + auto-review + auto-fix loop
#   claude-agent-runner --sequenced               Only pick up issues whose blockers are done
#   claude-agent-runner --pipeline <file.md>      Parse requirements into Linear issues
#   claude-agent-runner --spec <idea.md>          Generate spec from idea, optionally create issues
#   claude-agent-runner --review-pr <num> --repo <path>  Standalone PR review
#   claude-agent-runner --cleanup                 Clean up merged worktrees
#   claude-agent-runner --status                  Show active agents and worktrees
#   claude-agent-runner --help                    Show usage
#
# Config: ~/.config/claude-agents/config.json
# Logs:   ~/.config/claude-agents/logs/
#
# Requires: jq, claude, git, gh, curl
# Optional: docker (for local_db), pnpm

set -uo pipefail

CONFIG="$HOME/.config/claude-agents/config.json"
LOG_DIR="$HOME/.config/claude-agents/logs"
LOCK_DIR="$HOME/.config/claude-agents/locks"
SECRETS="$HOME/.config/claude-agents/secrets.env"

mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR" "$LOCK_DIR"

# Source secrets (API keys) — works in cron/background where ~/.zshrc isn't loaded
if [ -f "$SECRETS" ]; then
  # shellcheck disable=SC1090
  source "$SECRETS"
fi

# Load config
if [ ! -f "$CONFIG" ]; then
  echo "Config not found: $CONFIG"
  exit 1
fi

LABEL=$(jq -r '.label // "agent"' "$CONFIG")
MAX_PARALLEL=$(jq -r '.max_parallel // 3' "$CONFIG")
MAX_TURNS=$(jq -r '.max_turns // 15' "$CONFIG")
MAX_CI_RETRIES=$(jq -r '.max_ci_retries // 3' "$CONFIG")
MAX_REVIEW_FIX_ITERATIONS=$(jq -r '.max_review_fix_iterations // 3' "$CONFIG")

# Orchestrator config
ORCH_ENABLED=$(jq -r '.orchestrator.enabled // false' "$CONFIG")
ORCH_LABEL=$(jq -r '.orchestrator.label // "Orchestrate"' "$CONFIG")
ORCH_AUTO=$(jq -r '.orchestrator.auto // false' "$CONFIG")
ORCH_MAX_SUBTASKS=$(jq -r '.orchestrator.max_subtasks // 8' "$CONFIG")
ORCH_MAX_TURNS=$(jq -r '.orchestrator.max_orchestrator_turns // 50' "$CONFIG")
ORCH_POLL_INTERVAL=$(jq -r '.orchestrator.poll_interval_seconds // 30' "$CONFIG")
ORCH_MAX_RETRIES=$(jq -r '.orchestrator.max_validation_retries // 2' "$CONFIG")

# Global flags (set by CLI parsing)
AUTO_REVIEW=false
AUTO_FIX=false
SEQUENCED=false

log() {
  echo "[$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')] $1"
}

# --- JSONL Event Tracing ---

TRACE_DIR="$HOME/.config/claude-agents/traces"
mkdir -p "$TRACE_DIR"

# Initialize tracing for an issue run
# Sets TRACE_RUN_ID and TRACE_FILE; exports them for subshells
init_trace() {
  local WORKSPACE="$1"
  local IDENTIFIER="$2"
  TRACE_RUN_ID="${IDENTIFIER}-$(date +%s)"
  TRACE_FILE="${TRACE_DIR}/${WORKSPACE}-${IDENTIFIER}.jsonl"
  export TRACE_RUN_ID TRACE_FILE
}

# Emit a single JSONL trace event
# Usage: trace_event <event_name> [key=value ...]
# No-op if TRACE_FILE is unset. Pure printf, no jq, no subshells beyond date.
trace_event() {
  [ -z "${TRACE_FILE:-}" ] && return 0
  local EVENT="$1"; shift
  local TS
  TS=$(date -u '+%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ')
  local DATA="{"
  local FIRST=true
  while [ $# -gt 0 ]; do
    local KEY="${1%%=*}"
    local VAL="${1#*=}"
    # Escape quotes in value
    VAL=$(printf '%s' "$VAL" | sed 's/\\/\\\\/g; s/"/\\"/g')
    if [ "$FIRST" = true ]; then
      DATA="${DATA}\"${KEY}\":\"${VAL}\""
      FIRST=false
    else
      DATA="${DATA},\"${KEY}\":\"${VAL}\""
    fi
    shift
  done
  DATA="${DATA}}"
  printf '{"ts":"%s","event":"%s","run_id":"%s","identifier":"%s","workspace":"%s","data":%s}\n' \
    "$TS" "$EVENT" "${TRACE_RUN_ID:-}" "${TRACE_IDENTIFIER:-}" "${TRACE_WORKSPACE:-}" "$DATA" \
    >> "$TRACE_FILE"
}

# --- Resolve ci-gate path ---

resolve_ci_gate() {
  # 1. Explicit config path
  local CONFIG_PATH
  CONFIG_PATH=$(jq -r '.ci_gate_path // empty' "$CONFIG")
  if [ -n "$CONFIG_PATH" ] && [ -x "$CONFIG_PATH" ]; then
    echo "$CONFIG_PATH"
    return 0
  fi

  # 2. Sibling to this script (both in ~/.local/bin/ or same dir)
  local SCRIPT_DIR
  SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
  if [ -x "$SCRIPT_DIR/ci-gate" ]; then
    echo "$SCRIPT_DIR/ci-gate"
    return 0
  fi

  # 3. PATH lookup
  local PATH_CI
  PATH_CI=$(command -v ci-gate 2>/dev/null)
  if [ -n "$PATH_CI" ] && [ -x "$PATH_CI" ]; then
    echo "$PATH_CI"
    return 0
  fi

  echo ""
}

# --- Linear API helpers (all take API_KEY as first arg) ---

linear_graphql() {
  local API_KEY="$1"
  local QUERY="$2"
  curl -s -X POST https://api.linear.app/graphql \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -H "Authorization: $API_KEY" \
    -d "{\"query\": $(echo "$QUERY" | jq -Rs .)}"
}

linear_comment() {
  local API_KEY="$1"
  local ISSUE_ID="$2"
  local BODY="$3"
  linear_graphql "$API_KEY" "mutation { commentCreate(input: { issueId: \"$ISSUE_ID\", body: $(echo "$BODY" | jq -Rs .) }) { success comment { id } } }" \
    | jq -r '.data.commentCreate.comment.id // empty'
}

linear_delete_comment() {
  local API_KEY="$1"
  local COMMENT_ID="$2"
  linear_graphql "$API_KEY" "mutation { commentDelete(id: \"$COMMENT_ID\") { success } }" >/dev/null 2>&1
}

linear_set_state() {
  local API_KEY="$1"
  local ISSUE_ID="$2"
  local STATE_NAME="$3"
  local TEAM_ID="$4"

  local STATE_ID
  STATE_ID=$(linear_graphql "$API_KEY" "{ workflowStates(filter: { name: { eq: \"$STATE_NAME\" }, team: { id: { eq: \"$TEAM_ID\" } } }) { nodes { id name } } }" \
    | jq -r '.data.workflowStates.nodes[0].id // empty')

  if [ -n "$STATE_ID" ]; then
    linear_graphql "$API_KEY" "mutation { issueUpdate(id: \"$ISSUE_ID\", input: { stateId: \"$STATE_ID\" }) { success } }"
  fi
}

linear_get_comments() {
  local API_KEY="$1"
  local ISSUE_ID="$2"
  linear_graphql "$API_KEY" "{ issue(id: \"$ISSUE_ID\") { comments { nodes { body createdAt user { name } } } } }" \
    | jq -r '.data.issue.comments.nodes | sort_by(.createdAt) | .[] | "[\(.user.name // "system")] \(.body)"' 2>/dev/null
}

linear_remove_label() {
  local API_KEY="$1"
  local ISSUE_ID="$2"
  local LABEL_ID="$3"

  local CURRENT_LABELS
  CURRENT_LABELS=$(linear_graphql "$API_KEY" "{ issue(id: \"$ISSUE_ID\") { labels { nodes { id } } } }" \
    | jq -r "[.data.issue.labels.nodes[].id | select(. != \"$LABEL_ID\")] | join(\",\")")

  local LABEL_ARRAY
  if [ -z "$CURRENT_LABELS" ]; then
    LABEL_ARRAY="[]"
  else
    LABEL_ARRAY=$(echo "$CURRENT_LABELS" | tr ',' '\n' | jq -R . | jq -s .)
  fi

  linear_graphql "$API_KEY" "mutation { issueUpdate(id: \"$ISSUE_ID\", input: { labelIds: $LABEL_ARRAY }) { success } }"
}

linear_add_label() {
  local API_KEY="$1"
  local ISSUE_ID="$2"
  local LABEL_ID="$3"

  local CURRENT_LABELS
  CURRENT_LABELS=$(linear_graphql "$API_KEY" "{ issue(id: \"$ISSUE_ID\") { labels { nodes { id } } } }" \
    | jq -r "[.data.issue.labels.nodes[].id] + [\"$LABEL_ID\"] | unique | join(\",\")")

  local LABEL_ARRAY
  LABEL_ARRAY=$(echo "$CURRENT_LABELS" | tr ',' '\n' | jq -R . | jq -s .)

  linear_graphql "$API_KEY" "mutation { issueUpdate(id: \"$ISSUE_ID\", input: { labelIds: $LABEL_ARRAY }) { success } }"
}

linear_create_sub_issue() {
  local API_KEY="$1"
  local TEAM_ID="$2"
  local PARENT_ID="$3"
  local TITLE="$4"
  local DESCRIPTION="$5"
  local LABEL_IDS="$6"

  linear_graphql "$API_KEY" "mutation {
    issueCreate(input: {
      teamId: \"$TEAM_ID\"
      parentId: \"$PARENT_ID\"
      title: $(echo "$TITLE" | jq -Rs .)
      description: $(echo "$DESCRIPTION" | jq -Rs .)
      labelIds: $LABEL_IDS
    }) {
      success
      issue { id identifier }
    }
  }"
}

linear_get_issue_state() {
  local API_KEY="$1"
  local ISSUE_ID="$2"

  linear_graphql "$API_KEY" "{ issue(id: \"$ISSUE_ID\") { state { name type } } }" \
    | jq -r '.data.issue.state.type // "unknown"'
}

linear_get_sub_issues() {
  local API_KEY="$1"
  local PARENT_ID="$2"

  linear_graphql "$API_KEY" "{ issue(id: \"$PARENT_ID\") { children { nodes { id identifier title state { name type } } } } }" \
    | jq -r '.data.issue.children.nodes // []'
}

# --- GitHub PR feedback helpers ---

gather_pr_feedback() {
  local REPO_DIR="$1"
  local BRANCH="$2"
  local FEEDBACK=""

  cd "$REPO_DIR" || return 0

  # Check if a PR exists for this branch
  local PR_NUMBER
  PR_NUMBER=$(gh pr view "$BRANCH" --json number --jq '.number' 2>/dev/null) || return 0
  [ -z "$PR_NUMBER" ] && return 0

  local OWNER_REPO
  OWNER_REPO=$(gh repo view --json nameWithOwner --jq '.nameWithOwner' 2>/dev/null) || return 0

  # Top-level PR comments
  local PR_COMMENTS
  PR_COMMENTS=$(gh pr view "$BRANCH" --json comments --jq '.comments[] | "[\(.author.login)] \(.body)"' 2>/dev/null)
  if [ -n "$PR_COMMENTS" ]; then
    FEEDBACK="${FEEDBACK}## PR Comments
${PR_COMMENTS}

"
  fi

  # Review summaries
  local REVIEWS
  REVIEWS=$(gh pr view "$BRANCH" --json reviews --jq '.reviews[] | select(.body != "") | "[\(.author.login) - \(.state)] \(.body)"' 2>/dev/null)
  if [ -n "$REVIEWS" ]; then
    FEEDBACK="${FEEDBACK}## Review Summaries
${REVIEWS}

"
  fi

  # Inline file-level comments (review comments on specific lines)
  local INLINE
  INLINE=$(gh api "repos/${OWNER_REPO}/pulls/${PR_NUMBER}/comments" --jq '.[] | "[\(.user.login)] \(.path):\(.line // .original_line) — \(.body)"' 2>/dev/null)
  if [ -n "$INLINE" ]; then
    FEEDBACK="${FEEDBACK}## Inline Review Comments
${INLINE}

"
  fi

  echo "$FEEDBACK"
}

# --- Slack notification helper ---

slack_notify() {
  local WEBHOOK_URL="$1"
  local MESSAGE="$2"
  [ -z "$WEBHOOK_URL" ] && return 0
  curl -s -X POST "$WEBHOOK_URL" -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
    -d "{\"text\": $(echo "$MESSAGE" | jq -Rs .)}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
}

# --- Resolve API key for a workspace ---

resolve_api_key() {
  local WORKSPACE="$1"

  # Check for env var name in config
  local ENV_VAR
  ENV_VAR=$(jq -r ".workspaces[\"$WORKSPACE\"].api_key_env // empty" "$CONFIG")
  if [ -n "$ENV_VAR" ]; then
    local KEY="${!ENV_VAR:-}"
    if [ -n "$KEY" ]; then
      echo "$KEY"
      return 0
    fi
    echo "WARNING: env var $ENV_VAR not set for workspace $WORKSPACE" >&2
  fi

  # Fallback: LINEAR_API_KEY_<WORKSPACE>
  local AUTO_VAR="LINEAR_API_KEY_$(echo "$WORKSPACE" | tr '[:lower:]-' '[:upper:]_')"
  local KEY="${!AUTO_VAR:-}"
  if [ -n "$KEY" ]; then
    echo "$KEY"
    return 0
  fi

  # Last fallback: global LINEAR_API_KEY
  if [ -n "${LINEAR_API_KEY:-}" ]; then
    echo "$LINEAR_API_KEY"
    return 0
  fi

  echo "ERROR: No API key found for workspace $WORKSPACE" >&2
  echo "  Set one of: $ENV_VAR, $AUTO_VAR, or LINEAR_API_KEY" >&2
  return 1
}

# --- Resolve Slack webhook for a workspace ---

resolve_slack_webhook() {
  local WORKSPACE="$1"

  local ENV_VAR
  ENV_VAR=$(jq -r ".workspaces[\"$WORKSPACE\"].slack_webhook_env // empty" "$CONFIG")
  if [ -n "$ENV_VAR" ]; then
    local URL="${!ENV_VAR:-}"
    if [ -n "$URL" ]; then
      echo "$URL"
      return 0
    fi
  fi

  local AUTO_VAR="SLACK_WEBHOOK_$(echo "$WORKSPACE" | tr '[:lower:]-' '[:upper:]_')"
  local URL="${!AUTO_VAR:-}"
  if [ -n "$URL" ]; then
    echo "$URL"
    return 0
  fi

  echo ""
}

# --- Docker-per-agent DB isolation ---

start_agent_db() {
  local WORKTREE="$1"
  local IDENTIFIER="$2"
  local REPO_DIR="$3"
  local DB_TYPE="${4:-none}"

  [ "$DB_TYPE" = "none" ] && return 0

  local REPO_NAME
  REPO_NAME=$(basename "$REPO_DIR")
  local CONTAINER="agent-db-${IDENTIFIER}"

  # On resume: check if container is already running
  if docker ps --format '{{.Names}}' 2>/dev/null | grep -qx "$CONTAINER"; then
    log "[$REPO_NAME] DB container $CONTAINER already running, reusing"
    local EXISTING_PORT
    EXISTING_PORT=$(docker port "$CONTAINER" 5432 2>/dev/null | head -1 | cut -d: -f2)
    if [ -n "$EXISTING_PORT" ]; then
      _write_db_env "$WORKTREE" "$EXISTING_PORT" "$DB_TYPE"
    fi
    return 0
  fi

  # Remove stopped container with same name if it exists
  docker rm -f "$CONTAINER" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

  log "[$REPO_NAME] Starting DB container for ${IDENTIFIER} (type: ${DB_TYPE})"

  # Start postgres container with random ephemeral port
  docker run -d \
    --name "$CONTAINER" \
    --label "claude-agent=true" \
    -e POSTGRES_USER=postgres \
    -e POSTGRES_PASSWORD=postgres \
    -e POSTGRES_DB=agent \
    -p 0:5432 \
    postgres:16-alpine >/dev/null 2>&1

  if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
    log "[$REPO_NAME] WARNING: Failed to start DB container for ${IDENTIFIER}"
    return 1
  fi

  # Read assigned port
  local DB_PORT
  DB_PORT=$(docker port "$CONTAINER" 5432 2>/dev/null | head -1 | cut -d: -f2)
  if [ -z "$DB_PORT" ]; then
    log "[$REPO_NAME] WARNING: Could not determine DB port for ${IDENTIFIER}"
    docker rm -f "$CONTAINER" >/dev/null 2>&1
    return 1
  fi

  # Wait for pg_isready (max 30s)
  local WAITED=0
  while [ $WAITED -lt 30 ]; do
    if docker exec "$CONTAINER" pg_isready -U postgres >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      break
    fi
    sleep 1
    WAITED=$((WAITED + 1))
  done

  if [ $WAITED -ge 30 ]; then
    log "[$REPO_NAME] WARNING: DB container for ${IDENTIFIER} not ready after 30s"
    docker rm -f "$CONTAINER" >/dev/null 2>&1
    return 1
  fi

  # Run migrations if present
  local MIGRATION_DIR=""
  if [ -d "$REPO_DIR/supabase/migrations" ]; then
    MIGRATION_DIR="$REPO_DIR/supabase/migrations"
  elif [ -d "$REPO_DIR/migrations" ]; then
    MIGRATION_DIR="$REPO_DIR/migrations"
  fi

  if [ -n "$MIGRATION_DIR" ]; then
    log "[$REPO_NAME] Running migrations from $MIGRATION_DIR"
    for sql_file in "$MIGRATION_DIR"/*.sql; do
      [ -f "$sql_file" ] || continue
      docker exec -i "$CONTAINER" psql -U postgres -d agent < "$sql_file" >/dev/null 2>&1 || {
        log "[$REPO_NAME] WARNING: Migration failed: $(basename "$sql_file")"
      }
    done
  fi

  _write_db_env "$WORKTREE" "$DB_PORT" "$DB_TYPE"
  log "[$REPO_NAME] DB running for ${IDENTIFIER} on port ${DB_PORT}"
  trace_event "db.started" "type=$DB_TYPE" "port=$DB_PORT"
  return 0
}

_write_db_env() {
  local WORKTREE="$1"
  local DB_PORT="$2"
  local DB_TYPE="$3"

  local DB_URL="postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:${DB_PORT}/agent"

  # Strip existing DB env vars if .env.local exists
  if [ -f "$WORKTREE/.env.local" ]; then
    grep -v '^DATABASE_URL=' "$WORKTREE/.env.local" \
      | grep -v '^DIRECT_URL=' \
      | grep -v '^NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=' \
      | grep -v '^SUPABASE_DB_URL=' \
      > "$WORKTREE/.env.local.tmp" || true
    mv "$WORKTREE/.env.local.tmp" "$WORKTREE/.env.local"
  fi

  echo "DATABASE_URL=${DB_URL}" >> "$WORKTREE/.env.local"
  echo "DIRECT_URL=${DB_URL}" >> "$WORKTREE/.env.local"

  if [ "$DB_TYPE" = "supabase" ]; then
    echo "NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=http://localhost:${DB_PORT}" >> "$WORKTREE/.env.local"
    echo "SUPABASE_DB_URL=${DB_URL}" >> "$WORKTREE/.env.local"
  fi
}

stop_agent_db() {
  local IDENTIFIER="$1"
  local CONTAINER="agent-db-${IDENTIFIER}"
  docker rm -f "$CONTAINER" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
  trace_event "db.stopped"
}

cleanup_agent_dbs() {
  # Remove stopped containers with claude-agent label
  local STOPPED
  STOPPED=$(docker ps -a --filter "label=claude-agent=true" --filter "status=exited" --format '{{.Names}}' 2>/dev/null)
  if [ -n "$STOPPED" ]; then
    echo "$STOPPED" | while read -r name; do
      docker rm -f "$name" >/dev/null 2>&1
      log "Cleaned up stopped DB container: $name"
    done
  fi
}

# --- Fetch issues labeled "agent" for a team ---

fetch_agent_issues() {
  local API_KEY="$1"
  local TEAM_NAME="$2"
  local LIMIT="$3"

  local QUERY
  if [ "$SEQUENCED" = true ]; then
    # Include relations to check for blockers
    QUERY="{
      issues(
        filter: {
          team: { name: { eq: \"$TEAM_NAME\" } }
          labels: { name: { eq: \"$LABEL\" } }
          state: { type: { nin: [\"completed\", \"canceled\"] } }
        }
        first: $LIMIT
      ) {
        nodes {
          id
          identifier
          title
          description
          team { id name }
          parent { id }
          labels { nodes { id name } }
          relations {
            nodes {
              type
              relatedIssue {
                id
                identifier
                state { type }
              }
            }
          }
        }
      }
    }"
  else
    QUERY="{
      issues(
        filter: {
          team: { name: { eq: \"$TEAM_NAME\" } }
          labels: { name: { eq: \"$LABEL\" } }
          state: { type: { nin: [\"completed\", \"canceled\"] } }
        }
        first: $LIMIT
      ) {
        nodes {
          id
          identifier
          title
          description
          team { id name }
          parent { id }
          labels { nodes { id name } }
        }
      }
    }"
  fi

  local RESULT
  RESULT=$(linear_graphql "$API_KEY" "$QUERY" | jq '.data.issues.nodes // []')

  if [ "$SEQUENCED" = true ]; then
    # Filter out issues that have incomplete blockers
    echo "$RESULT" | jq '[.[] | select(
      ([.relations.nodes[]? | select(.type == "isBlockedBy") | .relatedIssue.state.type] |
       map(select(. != "completed" and . != "canceled")) | length) == 0
    )]'
  else
    echo "$RESULT"
  fi
}

# --- Worktree management ---

cleanup_worktrees() {
  local REPO_DIR="$1"
  local REPO_NAME
  REPO_NAME=$(basename "$REPO_DIR")
  cd "$REPO_DIR" || return 1

  local WORKTREE_BASE="$HOME/.claude/worktrees/${REPO_NAME}"
  local CLEANED=0
  if [ -d "$WORKTREE_BASE" ]; then
    for wt in "$WORKTREE_BASE"/issue-*; do
      [ -d "$wt" ] || continue
      local ISSUE_ID
      ISSUE_ID=$(basename "$wt" | sed 's/issue-//')

      local PR_STATE
      PR_STATE=$(gh pr list --head "agent/${ISSUE_ID}" --state all --json state --jq '.[0].state // "none"' 2>/dev/null)

      if [ "$PR_STATE" = "MERGED" ] || [ "$PR_STATE" = "CLOSED" ]; then
        log "[$REPO_NAME] Cleaning up worktree for ${ISSUE_ID} (PR ${PR_STATE})"

        # Stop DB container if running
        stop_agent_db "$ISSUE_ID"

        git worktree remove "$wt" --force 2>/dev/null || true
        git branch -D "agent/${ISSUE_ID}" 2>/dev/null || true
        CLEANED=$((CLEANED + 1))
        # Note: trace_event is no-op here since TRACE_FILE is not set in cleanup context
      fi
    done
  fi

  git worktree prune 2>/dev/null || true
  cleanup_agent_dbs
  [ "$CLEANED" -gt 0 ] && log "[$REPO_NAME] Cleaned $CLEANED worktrees"
}

show_status() {
  echo "=== Claude Agent Runner Status ==="
  echo ""

  local WORKSPACES
  WORKSPACES=$(jq -r '.workspaces | keys[]' "$CONFIG")

  while IFS= read -r WS; do
    echo "Workspace: $WS"

    local API_KEY
    API_KEY=$(resolve_api_key "$WS" 2>/dev/null) || { echo "  No API key configured"; echo ""; continue; }

    local TEAMS
    TEAMS=$(jq -r ".workspaces[\"$WS\"].teams | to_entries[] | \"\(.key)|\(.value.repo)|\(.value.local_db // \"none\")\"" "$CONFIG")

    while IFS='|' read -r TEAM_NAME REPO_DIR LOCAL_DB; do
      local REPO_NAME
      REPO_NAME=$(basename "$REPO_DIR")
      echo "  Team: $TEAM_NAME → $REPO_NAME (db: ${LOCAL_DB})"

      local ACTIVE=0
      local WT_BASE="$HOME/.claude/worktrees/$(basename "$REPO_DIR")"
      if [ -d "$WT_BASE" ]; then
        for wt in "$WT_BASE"/issue-*; do
          [ -d "$wt" ] || continue
          local ID
          ID=$(basename "$wt" | sed 's/issue-//')
          echo "    Active: $ID"
          ACTIVE=$((ACTIVE + 1))
        done
      fi
      [ "$ACTIVE" -eq 0 ] && echo "    No active agents"

      local PENDING
      PENDING=$(fetch_agent_issues "$API_KEY" "$TEAM_NAME" 10 | jq length)
      echo "    Pending: $PENDING issues labeled '$LABEL'"
    done <<< "$TEAMS"
    echo ""
  done <<< "$WORKSPACES"
}

# --- Auto-review agent (Feature 2) ---

auto_review_pr() {
  local REPO_DIR="$1"
  local BRANCH="$2"
  local IDENTIFIER="$3"

  cd "$REPO_DIR" || return 1

  local PR_NUMBER
  PR_NUMBER=$(gh pr view "$BRANCH" --json number --jq '.number' 2>/dev/null) || return 1
  [ -z "$PR_NUMBER" ] && return 1

  log "[review] Reviewing PR #${PR_NUMBER} for ${IDENTIFIER}"
  trace_event "review.started" "pr_number=$PR_NUMBER"

  # Get the full diff
  local DIFF
  DIFF=$(gh pr diff "$BRANCH" 2>/dev/null)
  [ -z "$DIFF" ] && { log "[review] No diff found for PR #${PR_NUMBER}"; return 1; }

  # Get PR info
  local PR_TITLE PR_BODY
  PR_TITLE=$(gh pr view "$BRANCH" --json title --jq '.title' 2>/dev/null)
  PR_BODY=$(gh pr view "$BRANCH" --json body --jq '.body' 2>/dev/null)

  # Create a temp file for the review prompt
  local REVIEW_PROMPT
  REVIEW_PROMPT=$(mktemp)
  cat > "$REVIEW_PROMPT" << REVIEW_EOF
You are a senior code reviewer. Review this PR thoroughly.

## PR: ${PR_TITLE}

### Description
${PR_BODY}

### Diff
\`\`\`diff
${DIFF}
\`\`\`

## Your task
Review the code and output ONLY a JSON object with this exact structure:
{
  "verdict": "approve" | "request_changes" | "comment",
  "summary": "1-2 sentence overall assessment",
  "blocking": ["list of issues that MUST be fixed before merge"],
  "important": ["list of issues that SHOULD be fixed"],
  "nits": ["minor style/preference suggestions"],
  "praise": ["things done well"]
}

Rules:
- Only use "request_changes" for genuine bugs, security issues, or broken functionality
- Style preferences and minor improvements should be "nits" or "important", not blocking
- Be specific — reference file names and line numbers
- Output ONLY the JSON, no markdown fences, no extra text
REVIEW_EOF

  # Spawn Claude in plan mode (read-only) for the review
  local REVIEW_OUTPUT
  REVIEW_OUTPUT=$(claude --permission-mode plan --print -p "$(cat "$REVIEW_PROMPT")" --max-turns 20 2>/dev/null)
  rm -f "$REVIEW_PROMPT"

  if [ -z "$REVIEW_OUTPUT" ]; then
    log "[review] Claude returned empty output for PR #${PR_NUMBER}"
    return 1
  fi

  # Try to parse as JSON
  local VERDICT SUMMARY REVIEW_BODY
  VERDICT=$(echo "$REVIEW_OUTPUT" | jq -r '.verdict // empty' 2>/dev/null)

  if [ -n "$VERDICT" ]; then
    SUMMARY=$(echo "$REVIEW_OUTPUT" | jq -r '.summary // ""' 2>/dev/null)
    local BLOCKING IMPORTANT NITS PRAISE
    BLOCKING=$(echo "$REVIEW_OUTPUT" | jq -r '.blocking // [] | map("- **BLOCKING**: " + .) | join("\n")' 2>/dev/null)
    IMPORTANT=$(echo "$REVIEW_OUTPUT" | jq -r '.important // [] | map("- **Important**: " + .) | join("\n")' 2>/dev/null)
    NITS=$(echo "$REVIEW_OUTPUT" | jq -r '.nits // [] | map("- _Nit_: " + .) | join("\n")' 2>/dev/null)
    PRAISE=$(echo "$REVIEW_OUTPUT" | jq -r '.praise // [] | map("- :thumbsup: " + .) | join("\n")' 2>/dev/null)

    REVIEW_BODY="## Auto-Review: ${SUMMARY}

${BLOCKING}
${IMPORTANT}
${NITS}
${PRAISE}

---
_Automated review by claude-agent-runner_"

    # Post review via gh
    case "$VERDICT" in
      approve)
        gh pr review "$PR_NUMBER" --approve --body "$REVIEW_BODY" 2>/dev/null
        log "[review] PR #${PR_NUMBER} APPROVED"
        ;;
      request_changes)
        gh pr review "$PR_NUMBER" --request-changes --body "$REVIEW_BODY" 2>/dev/null
        log "[review] PR #${PR_NUMBER} CHANGES REQUESTED"
        ;;
      *)
        gh pr review "$PR_NUMBER" --comment --body "$REVIEW_BODY" 2>/dev/null
        log "[review] PR #${PR_NUMBER} COMMENTED"
        ;;
    esac
  else
    # Non-JSON output — post as plain comment
    gh pr review "$PR_NUMBER" --comment --body "## Auto-Review

${REVIEW_OUTPUT}

---
_Automated review by claude-agent-runner_" 2>/dev/null
    VERDICT="comment"
    log "[review] PR #${PR_NUMBER} commented (non-JSON output)"
  fi

  trace_event "review.completed" "verdict=$VERDICT" "pr_number=$PR_NUMBER"
  echo "$VERDICT"
}

# --- Auto-fix loop (Feature 3) ---

auto_fix_loop() {
  local WORKTREE="$1"
  local REPO_DIR="$2"
  local BRANCH="$3"
  local IDENTIFIER="$4"
  local REPO_NAME="$5"

  cd "$WORKTREE" || return 1

  local CI_GATE
  CI_GATE=$(resolve_ci_gate)

  for ITER in $(seq 1 "$MAX_REVIEW_FIX_ITERATIONS"); do
    log "[auto-fix] Iteration ${ITER}/${MAX_REVIEW_FIX_ITERATIONS} for ${IDENTIFIER}"
    trace_event "fix.started" "iteration=$ITER"

    # Check latest review verdict
    local LATEST_REVIEW_STATE
    LATEST_REVIEW_STATE=$(cd "$REPO_DIR" && gh pr view "$BRANCH" --json reviews --jq '[.reviews[] | select(.state != "")] | last | .state // "NONE"' 2>/dev/null)

    if [ "$LATEST_REVIEW_STATE" = "APPROVED" ]; then
      log "[auto-fix] PR already approved, stopping fix loop"
      return 0
    fi

    if [ "$LATEST_REVIEW_STATE" != "CHANGES_REQUESTED" ]; then
      log "[auto-fix] No changes requested (state: ${LATEST_REVIEW_STATE}), stopping fix loop"
      return 0
    fi

    # Gather all feedback
    local PR_FEEDBACK
    PR_FEEDBACK=$(gather_pr_feedback "$REPO_DIR" "$BRANCH")

    if [ -z "$PR_FEEDBACK" ]; then
      log "[auto-fix] No feedback to address, stopping"
      return 0
    fi

    # Spawn fix agent
    cd "$WORKTREE" || return 1

    # Export CLAUDE_AGENT_MODE for guardrail hook
    export CLAUDE_AGENT_MODE=1
    export CLAUDE_AGENT_REPO="$REPO_NAME"
    export CLAUDE_AGENT_BRANCH="$BRANCH"
    export CLAUDE_AGENT_ISSUE_ID="$IDENTIFIER"
    export CLAUDE_AGENT_WORKTREE="$WORKTREE"

    claude --permission-mode bypassPermissions --setting-sources project,local -p "You are working in an isolated git worktree for the ${REPO_NAME} project.
Read CLAUDE.md for all project conventions.

## Fix PR Feedback: ${IDENTIFIER}

Address ALL reviewer feedback below. Make new, atomic commits for each fix.
Do NOT amend existing commits or force-push.
Do NOT push or open a PR — that is handled externally.

${PR_FEEDBACK}

Fix every issue mentioned above." --max-turns 500 >> "$LOG_DIR/${IDENTIFIER}-fix-${ITER}.log" 2>&1

    unset CLAUDE_AGENT_MODE

    # Run CI gate
    if [ -n "$CI_GATE" ] && [ -x "$CI_GATE" ]; then
      if ! "$CI_GATE" "$WORKTREE" >> "$LOG_DIR/${IDENTIFIER}-fix-ci-${ITER}.log" 2>&1; then
        log "[auto-fix] CI failed after fix iteration ${ITER}"
        # One CI retry within the fix
        claude --permission-mode bypassPermissions --setting-sources project,local -p "CI gate failed after fixing PR feedback. Fix the CI errors:

$(cat "$LOG_DIR/${IDENTIFIER}-fix-ci-${ITER}.log" | tail -50)

Make new commits. Do NOT push." --max-turns 200 >> "$LOG_DIR/${IDENTIFIER}-fix-ci-retry-${ITER}.log" 2>&1

        if ! "$CI_GATE" "$WORKTREE" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          log "[auto-fix] CI still failing after retry, bailing out of fix loop"
          trace_event "fix.ci_failed" "iteration=$ITER"
          return 1
        fi
      fi
    fi

    # Push new commits
    cd "$WORKTREE" || return 1
    git push origin "$BRANCH" 2>&1

    # Re-run auto-review
    local NEW_VERDICT
    NEW_VERDICT=$(auto_review_pr "$REPO_DIR" "$BRANCH" "$IDENTIFIER")

    if [ "$NEW_VERDICT" = "approve" ]; then
      log "[auto-fix] PR approved after fix iteration ${ITER}"
      trace_event "fix.completed" "iteration=$ITER" "verdict=approve"
      return 0
    fi

    log "[auto-fix] Review verdict after fix ${ITER}: ${NEW_VERDICT}"
  done

  log "[auto-fix] Exhausted ${MAX_REVIEW_FIX_ITERATIONS} fix iterations for ${IDENTIFIER}"
  return 1
}

# --- Orchestrator (multi-agent decomposition) ---

# Check if an issue should be orchestrated (by label)
should_orchestrate() {
  local ISSUE_JSON="$1"

  # Check if the issue has the orchestrate label
  local HAS_ORCH_LABEL
  HAS_ORCH_LABEL=$(echo "$ISSUE_JSON" | jq -r --arg label "$ORCH_LABEL" \
    '.labels.nodes[]? | select(.name == $label) | .name' 2>/dev/null)

  [ -n "$HAS_ORCH_LABEL" ]
}

# Check if an issue is an orchestrated subtask (has a parent issue)
is_orchestrated_subtask() {
  local ISSUE_JSON="$1"

  local HAS_PARENT
  HAS_PARENT=$(echo "$ISSUE_JSON" | jq -r '.parent.id // empty' 2>/dev/null)

  [ -n "$HAS_PARENT" ]
}

# Orchestrate a parent issue: decompose into sub-issues and supervise
orchestrate_issue() {
  local WORKTREE="$1"
  local REPO_DIR="$2"
  local IDENTIFIER="$3"
  local TITLE="$4"
  local DESCRIPTION="$5"
  local API_KEY="$6"
  local ISSUE_ID="$7"
  local TEAM_ID="$8"
  local WORKSPACE="$9"
  local TEAM_NAME="${10}"
  local SLACK_WEBHOOK="${11}"
  local REPO_NAME
  REPO_NAME=$(basename "$REPO_DIR")

  local ORCH_DIR="$WORKTREE/.claude/orchestrator"
  mkdir -p "$ORCH_DIR"

  log "[orchestrator] Decomposing ${IDENTIFIER}: ${TITLE}"
  linear_comment "$API_KEY" "$ISSUE_ID" "🧩 **Orchestrator started** — decomposing into subtasks" >/dev/null 2>&1
  slack_notify "$SLACK_WEBHOOK" "🧩 [${WORKSPACE}/${TEAM_NAME}] Orchestrator started for ${IDENTIFIER}: ${TITLE}"

  cd "$WORKTREE"

  # Phase 1: Spawn Claude to decompose the ticket
  export CLAUDE_AGENT_MODE=1
  export CLAUDE_AGENT_REPO="$REPO_NAME"
  export CLAUDE_AGENT_ISSUE_ID="$IDENTIFIER"
  export CLAUDE_AGENT_WORKTREE="$WORKTREE"
  unset CLAUDECODE 2>/dev/null || true

  claude --permission-mode bypassPermissions --setting-sources project,local -p "You are an orchestrator decomposing a Linear issue into subtasks.

Read CLAUDE.md for project conventions and architecture.

## Parent Issue: ${IDENTIFIER}
**Title:** ${TITLE}

**Description:**
${DESCRIPTION}

## Instructions

Use the orchestrate skill to decompose this issue into scoped subtasks.
Write subtask JSON files to .claude/orchestrator/subtask-NNN.json

If this issue is single-concern and doesn't need decomposition, write:
{\"no_decomposition\": true, \"reason\": \"...\"}
to .claude/orchestrator/no_decomposition.json

Max subtasks: ${ORCH_MAX_SUBTASKS}

Explore the codebase thoroughly before decomposing. Every file path in your scope assignments must be a real path." --max-turns "$ORCH_MAX_TURNS" >> "$LOG_DIR/${WORKSPACE}-${IDENTIFIER}-orchestrate.log" 2>&1

  # Check for no-decomposition result
  if [ -f "$ORCH_DIR/no_decomposition.json" ]; then
    log "[orchestrator] ${IDENTIFIER} — no decomposition needed"
    trace_event "orchestrator.fallback" "reason=no_decomposition"
    linear_comment "$API_KEY" "$ISSUE_ID" "🧩 Orchestrator decided no decomposition needed — falling back to single agent" >/dev/null 2>&1
    return 1  # Signal caller to fall back to single-agent
  fi

  # Read subtask files
  local SUBTASK_COUNT=0
  local SUBTASK_FILES=()
  for f in "$ORCH_DIR"/subtask-*.json; do
    [ -f "$f" ] || continue
    if jq empty "$f" 2>/dev/null; then
      SUBTASK_FILES+=("$f")
      SUBTASK_COUNT=$((SUBTASK_COUNT + 1))
    else
      log "[orchestrator] WARNING: Invalid JSON in $(basename "$f"), skipping"
    fi
  done

  if [ "$SUBTASK_COUNT" -le 1 ]; then
    log "[orchestrator] ${IDENTIFIER} — ${SUBTASK_COUNT} subtasks (need 2+), falling back to single agent"
    trace_event "orchestrator.fallback" "reason=too_few_subtasks" "count=$SUBTASK_COUNT"
    linear_comment "$API_KEY" "$ISSUE_ID" "🧩 Orchestrator produced ${SUBTASK_COUNT} subtask(s) — falling back to single agent" >/dev/null 2>&1
    return 1
  fi

  log "[orchestrator] ${IDENTIFIER} decomposed into ${SUBTASK_COUNT} subtasks"
  trace_event "orchestrator.decomposed" "count=$SUBTASK_COUNT"

  # Get agent label ID
  local AGENT_LABEL_ID
  AGENT_LABEL_ID=$(linear_graphql "$API_KEY" "{ issueLabels(filter: { name: { eq: \"$LABEL\" } }) { nodes { id } } }" \
    | jq -r '.data.issueLabels.nodes[0].id // empty')

  local LABEL_IDS="[]"
  [ -n "$AGENT_LABEL_ID" ] && LABEL_IDS="[\"$AGENT_LABEL_ID\"]"

  # Phase 2: Create sub-issues in Linear
  # Build state.json as we go
  local STATE_SUBTASKS="[]"

  for subtask_file in "${SUBTASK_FILES[@]}"; do
    local ST_ID ST_TITLE ST_SKILL ST_SCOPE ST_OFF_LIMITS ST_SHARED ST_DEPS ST_CRITERIA ST_CONTEXT
    ST_ID=$(jq -r '.id' "$subtask_file")
    ST_TITLE=$(jq -r '.title' "$subtask_file")
    ST_SKILL=$(jq -r '.skill // "implement"' "$subtask_file")
    ST_SCOPE=$(jq -r '.scope | join(", ")' "$subtask_file")
    ST_CRITERIA=$(jq -r '.acceptance_criteria | map("- " + .) | join("\n")' "$subtask_file")
    ST_CONTEXT=$(jq -r '.context // ""' "$subtask_file")
    ST_DEPS=$(jq -r '.depends_on // [] | join(", ")' "$subtask_file")

    # Build sub-issue description with scope metadata
    local SUB_DESC="## Subtask of ${IDENTIFIER}: ${TITLE}

${ST_CONTEXT}

## File Scope

**Allowed (write):** ${ST_SCOPE}

**Off limits:** $(jq -r '.off_limits | join(", ")' "$subtask_file")

**Shared files (read-only):** $(jq -r '.shared_files // [] | join(", ")' "$subtask_file")

## Acceptance Criteria

${ST_CRITERIA}

## Dependencies

${ST_DEPS:-None}
"

    # Create the sub-issue
    local CREATE_RESULT
    CREATE_RESULT=$(linear_create_sub_issue "$API_KEY" "$TEAM_ID" "$ISSUE_ID" \
      "[${ST_ID}] ${ST_TITLE}" "$SUB_DESC" "$LABEL_IDS")

    local SUB_ISSUE_ID SUB_IDENTIFIER
    SUB_ISSUE_ID=$(echo "$CREATE_RESULT" | jq -r '.data.issueCreate.issue.id // empty')
    SUB_IDENTIFIER=$(echo "$CREATE_RESULT" | jq -r '.data.issueCreate.issue.identifier // empty')

    if [ -n "$SUB_ISSUE_ID" ]; then
      log "[orchestrator] Created subtask: ${SUB_IDENTIFIER} — ${ST_TITLE}"
      trace_event "subtask.created" "subtask_id=$ST_ID" "identifier=$SUB_IDENTIFIER" "title=$ST_TITLE"

      # Track in state
      STATE_SUBTASKS=$(echo "$STATE_SUBTASKS" | jq \
        --arg id "$ST_ID" \
        --arg linear_id "$SUB_ISSUE_ID" \
        --arg linear_ident "$SUB_IDENTIFIER" \
        --arg skill "$ST_SKILL" \
        '. + [{"id": $id, "linear_id": $linear_id, "linear_identifier": $linear_ident, "status": "pending", "skill": $skill}]')
    else
      log "[orchestrator] ERROR: Failed to create subtask: ${ST_TITLE}"
    fi
  done

  # Create dependency relations between sub-issues
  for subtask_file in "${SUBTASK_FILES[@]}"; do
    local ST_ID DEPS
    ST_ID=$(jq -r '.id' "$subtask_file")
    DEPS=$(jq -r '.depends_on[]?' "$subtask_file" 2>/dev/null)
    [ -z "$DEPS" ] && continue

    # Find this subtask's Linear ID
    local THIS_LINEAR_ID
    THIS_LINEAR_ID=$(echo "$STATE_SUBTASKS" | jq -r --arg id "$ST_ID" '.[] | select(.id == $id) | .linear_id')
    [ -z "$THIS_LINEAR_ID" ] && continue

    # Don't add agent label to subtasks with dependencies (they'll get it when unblocked)
    if [ -n "$AGENT_LABEL_ID" ]; then
      linear_remove_label "$API_KEY" "$THIS_LINEAR_ID" "$AGENT_LABEL_ID" >/dev/null 2>&1
    fi

    while IFS= read -r dep_id; do
      local BLOCKER_LINEAR_ID
      BLOCKER_LINEAR_ID=$(echo "$STATE_SUBTASKS" | jq -r --arg id "$dep_id" '.[] | select(.id == $id) | .linear_id')
      if [ -n "$BLOCKER_LINEAR_ID" ]; then
        linear_graphql "$API_KEY" "mutation {
          issueRelationCreate(input: {
            issueId: \"$THIS_LINEAR_ID\"
            relatedIssueId: \"$BLOCKER_LINEAR_ID\"
            type: \"isBlockedBy\"
          }) { success }
        }" >/dev/null 2>&1
        log "[orchestrator] Linked: subtask ${ST_ID} blocked by ${dep_id}"
      fi
    done <<< "$DEPS"
  done

  # Write state.json
  local SHARED_FILES
  SHARED_FILES=$(jq -s '[.[].shared_files // [] | .[]] | unique' "${SUBTASK_FILES[@]}" 2>/dev/null || echo "[]")

  jq -n \
    --arg parent_id "$IDENTIFIER" \
    --arg branch "agent/${IDENTIFIER}" \
    --arg issue_id "$ISSUE_ID" \
    --argjson subtasks "$STATE_SUBTASKS" \
    --argjson shared_files "$SHARED_FILES" \
    '{
      parent_id: $parent_id,
      branch: $branch,
      issue_id: $issue_id,
      subtasks: $subtasks,
      shared_files: $shared_files,
      created_at: (now | todate),
      status: "active"
    }' > "$ORCH_DIR/state.json"

  # Post summary to parent issue
  local SUBTASK_SUMMARY=""
  for subtask_file in "${SUBTASK_FILES[@]}"; do
    local ST_ID ST_TITLE
    ST_ID=$(jq -r '.id' "$subtask_file")
    ST_TITLE=$(jq -r '.title' "$subtask_file")
    local ST_IDENT
    ST_IDENT=$(echo "$STATE_SUBTASKS" | jq -r --arg id "$ST_ID" '.[] | select(.id == $id) | .linear_identifier')
    SUBTASK_SUMMARY="${SUBTASK_SUMMARY}
- **${ST_IDENT}** [${ST_ID}] ${ST_TITLE}"
  done

  linear_comment "$API_KEY" "$ISSUE_ID" "🧩 **Orchestrator decomposed into ${SUBTASK_COUNT} subtasks:**
${SUBTASK_SUMMARY}

Workers will pick up subtasks as they become unblocked. The orchestrator will validate results and create a combined PR." >/dev/null 2>&1

  # Commit the orchestrator state
  cd "$WORKTREE"
  git add .claude/orchestrator/ 2>/dev/null
  git commit -m "chore: orchestrator decomposition for ${IDENTIFIER}" 2>/dev/null || true

  # Phase 3: Supervisor loop — poll for sub-issue completion
  log "[orchestrator] Entering supervisor loop for ${IDENTIFIER}"

  while true; do
    sleep "$ORCH_POLL_INTERVAL"

    # Read current state
    if [ ! -f "$ORCH_DIR/state.json" ]; then
      log "[orchestrator] ERROR: state.json missing, aborting"
      break
    fi

    # Check sub-issue states in Linear
    local ALL_DONE=true
    local ANY_FAILED=false
    local UPDATED_SUBTASKS="$STATE_SUBTASKS"

    for subtask_entry in $(echo "$STATE_SUBTASKS" | jq -c '.[]'); do
      local ST_ID ST_LINEAR_ID ST_STATUS
      ST_ID=$(echo "$subtask_entry" | jq -r '.id')
      ST_LINEAR_ID=$(echo "$subtask_entry" | jq -r '.linear_id')
      ST_STATUS=$(echo "$subtask_entry" | jq -r '.status')

      # Skip already-completed subtasks
      [ "$ST_STATUS" = "completed" ] && continue
      [ "$ST_STATUS" = "validated" ] && continue

      # Query Linear for current state
      local LINEAR_STATE
      LINEAR_STATE=$(linear_get_issue_state "$API_KEY" "$ST_LINEAR_ID")

      case "$LINEAR_STATE" in
        completed)
          log "[orchestrator] Subtask ${ST_ID} completed — validating"
          trace_event "subtask.completed" "subtask_id=$ST_ID"
          UPDATED_SUBTASKS=$(echo "$UPDATED_SUBTASKS" | jq --arg id "$ST_ID" \
            '[.[] | if .id == $id then .status = "completed" else . end]')

          # Check for scope overflow
          local OVERFLOW_FILE="$ORCH_DIR/scope-overflow-${ST_ID}.md"
          if [ -f "$OVERFLOW_FILE" ]; then
            log "[orchestrator] Subtask ${ST_ID} has scope overflow — creating follow-up"
            trace_event "subtask.scope_overflow" "subtask_id=$ST_ID"
            local OVERFLOW_CONTENT
            OVERFLOW_CONTENT=$(cat "$OVERFLOW_FILE")
            linear_comment "$API_KEY" "$ISSUE_ID" "⚠️ Subtask ${ST_ID} reported scope overflow:

${OVERFLOW_CONTENT}

Creating a follow-up subtask." >/dev/null 2>&1
            # Overflow handling: could create another sub-issue here
            # For now, log it for the orchestrator's final validation
          fi
          ;;
        canceled)
          log "[orchestrator] Subtask ${ST_ID} was canceled"
          UPDATED_SUBTASKS=$(echo "$UPDATED_SUBTASKS" | jq --arg id "$ST_ID" \
            '[.[] | if .id == $id then .status = "canceled" else . end]')
          ;;
        *)
          ALL_DONE=false
          ;;
      esac
    done

    STATE_SUBTASKS="$UPDATED_SUBTASKS"

    # Update state.json
    jq --argjson subtasks "$STATE_SUBTASKS" '.subtasks = $subtasks' "$ORCH_DIR/state.json" > "$ORCH_DIR/state.json.tmp" \
      && mv "$ORCH_DIR/state.json.tmp" "$ORCH_DIR/state.json"

    if [ "$ALL_DONE" = true ]; then
      break
    fi
  done

  log "[orchestrator] All subtasks completed for ${IDENTIFIER} — running integration validation"

  # Phase 4: Integration validation
  cd "$WORKTREE"

  # Pull latest changes from sub-issue workers (they commit to the same branch)
  git pull origin "agent/${IDENTIFIER}" 2>/dev/null || true

  # Run CI gate
  local CI_GATE
  CI_GATE=$(resolve_ci_gate)

  local INTEGRATION_PASSED=false
  trace_event "integration.started"
  if [ -n "$CI_GATE" ] && [ -x "$CI_GATE" ]; then
    if "$CI_GATE" "$WORKTREE" >> "$LOG_DIR/${WORKSPACE}-${IDENTIFIER}-integration.log" 2>&1; then
      INTEGRATION_PASSED=true
      log "[orchestrator] Integration CI passed for ${IDENTIFIER}"
      trace_event "integration.passed"
    else
      log "[orchestrator] Integration CI failed for ${IDENTIFIER} — spawning fix agent"
      trace_event "integration.failed"

      local CI_ERRORS
      CI_ERRORS=$(cat "$LOG_DIR/${WORKSPACE}-${IDENTIFIER}-integration.log" | tail -80)

      claude --permission-mode bypassPermissions --setting-sources project,local -p "You are working in an isolated git worktree for the ${REPO_NAME} project.
Read CLAUDE.md for project conventions.

## Integration CI Failed — Fix These Errors

This is an orchestrated feature (${IDENTIFIER}: ${TITLE}) with multiple subtasks that have been completed.
The integration CI gate failed. Fix ALL errors below.

## CI Output:
\`\`\`
${CI_ERRORS}
\`\`\`

Commit fixes with: fix: resolve integration CI failures for ${IDENTIFIER}
Do NOT push or open a PR." --max-turns 500 >> "$LOG_DIR/${WORKSPACE}-${IDENTIFIER}-integration-fix.log" 2>&1

      # Retry CI
      if "$CI_GATE" "$WORKTREE" >> "$LOG_DIR/${WORKSPACE}-${IDENTIFIER}-integration-retry.log" 2>&1; then
        INTEGRATION_PASSED=true
        log "[orchestrator] Integration CI passed after fix for ${IDENTIFIER}"
        trace_event "integration.passed" "after_fix=true"
      else
        log "[orchestrator] Integration CI still failing for ${IDENTIFIER}"
        trace_event "integration.failed" "after_fix=true"
      fi
    fi
  else
    INTEGRATION_PASSED=true  # No CI gate = pass
  fi

  # Phase 5: Create PR
  if [ "$INTEGRATION_PASSED" = true ]; then
    local TITLE_SLUG
    TITLE_SLUG=$(echo "$TITLE" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]' | sed 's/[^a-z0-9]/-/g; s/--*/-/g; s/^-//; s/-$//' | cut -c1-50)
    local BRANCH="agent/${IDENTIFIER}-${TITLE_SLUG}"
    git push -u origin "$BRANCH" 2>&1

    # Build PR body from all agent reports
    local PR_BODY="## ${IDENTIFIER}: ${TITLE}

### Orchestrated Implementation

This feature was decomposed into ${SUBTASK_COUNT} subtasks by the orchestrator.

### Subtask Reports
"
    for subtask_entry in $(echo "$STATE_SUBTASKS" | jq -c '.[]'); do
      local ST_ID ST_IDENT
      ST_ID=$(echo "$subtask_entry" | jq -r '.id')
      ST_IDENT=$(echo "$subtask_entry" | jq -r '.linear_identifier')
      local REPORT_FILE="$WORKTREE/.claude/agent-reports/${ST_IDENT}.md"
      if [ -f "$REPORT_FILE" ]; then
        PR_BODY="${PR_BODY}
#### ${ST_IDENT} (subtask ${ST_ID})
$(cat "$REPORT_FILE")

"
      fi
    done

    PR_BODY="${PR_BODY}
---
*Implemented by orchestrated Claude agents. CI gate passed.*
Closes ${IDENTIFIER}"

    local PR_URL
    PR_URL=$(gh pr create \
      --draft \
      --title "${IDENTIFIER}: ${TITLE}" \
      --body "$PR_BODY" \
      --head "$BRANCH" \
      --base main 2>&1 | tail -1 | grep -oE 'https://github\.com/[^ ]+')

    if [ -n "$PR_URL" ]; then
      linear_set_state "$API_KEY" "$ISSUE_ID" "In Review" "$TEAM_ID" >/dev/null 2>&1
      linear_comment "$API_KEY" "$ISSUE_ID" "✅ **Orchestrator completed** — all ${SUBTASK_COUNT} subtasks done, CI passed, PR opened: ${PR_URL}" >/dev/null 2>&1
      slack_notify "$SLACK_WEBHOOK" "✅ [${WORKSPACE}/${TEAM_NAME}] Orchestrated ${IDENTIFIER} completed — ${SUBTASK_COUNT} subtasks, PR: ${PR_URL}"
      log "[orchestrator] ${IDENTIFIER} done — PR: $PR_URL"
    else
      linear_comment "$API_KEY" "$ISSUE_ID" "⚠️ **Orchestrator push succeeded but PR creation failed** — check logs" >/dev/null 2>&1
      log "[orchestrator] ${IDENTIFIER} pushed but PR creation failed"
    fi
  else
    linear_comment "$API_KEY" "$ISSUE_ID" "❌ **Orchestrator integration CI failed** — needs human intervention.

Check logs: ${LOG_DIR}/${WORKSPACE}-${IDENTIFIER}-integration*.log" >/dev/null 2>&1
    slack_notify "$SLACK_WEBHOOK" "❌ [${WORKSPACE}/${TEAM_NAME}] Orchestrated ${IDENTIFIER} integration CI failed — needs human intervention"
    log "[orchestrator] ${IDENTIFIER} integration failed"
  fi

  # Update state
  jq '.status = "completed"' "$ORCH_DIR/state.json" > "$ORCH_DIR/state.json.tmp" \
    && mv "$ORCH_DIR/state.json.tmp" "$ORCH_DIR/state.json"

  return 0
}

# Run a scoped worker for an orchestrated subtask
run_scoped_worker() {
  local WORKTREE="$1"
  local REPO_NAME="$2"
  local IDENTIFIER="$3"
  local TITLE="$4"
  local DESCRIPTION="$5"

  # Find the subtask spec embedded in the description
  local SCOPE_SECTION
  SCOPE_SECTION=$(echo "$DESCRIPTION" | sed -n '/## File Scope/,/## Acceptance/p' | head -n -1)

  local CRITERIA_SECTION
  CRITERIA_SECTION=$(echo "$DESCRIPTION" | sed -n '/## Acceptance Criteria/,/## Dependencies/p' | head -n -1)

  # Create a temporary scope file for the scope-guard hook
  local SCOPE_FILE="$WORKTREE/.claude/orchestrator/active-scope.json"
  mkdir -p "$(dirname "$SCOPE_FILE")"

  # Extract scope from the description — parse "Allowed (write):" line
  local WRITE_SCOPE
  WRITE_SCOPE=$(echo "$SCOPE_SECTION" | grep -A1 'Allowed (write):' | tail -1 | sed 's/^ *//')

  # Build a simple scope JSON from the comma-separated list
  echo "$WRITE_SCOPE" | tr ',' '\n' | sed 's/^ *//;s/ *$//' | jq -R . | jq -s '{scope: .}' > "$SCOPE_FILE"

  # Set scoped worker environment
  export CLAUDE_AGENT_SCOPED=1
  export CLAUDE_AGENT_SCOPE_FILE="$SCOPE_FILE"
  export CLAUDE_AGENT_MODE=1
  export CLAUDE_AGENT_REPO="$REPO_NAME"
  export CLAUDE_AGENT_ISSUE_ID="$IDENTIFIER"
  export CLAUDE_AGENT_WORKTREE="$WORKTREE"
